rbvh41ja05sk3hr0lmv15v4h9k.summary: Review your last discussion with Randy from May 15—focusing on family updates, the Alithia project kickoff, and outstanding audits. Address the Alithia headwinds—specifically the loss of legacy business rules due to retirements—and coordinate with Yousef to navigate the Oracle data schemas. Track the investigation into the $6 million clearing account drift—a recurring variance between FinPal and Base caused by direct journal entries.
tasks: Ask Randy for status updates on locating the root cause of the $6 million FinPal and Base clearing account drift.
Verify whether the business team has made progress in documenting the legacy reserve allocation rules for the Alithia project.
Determine if Yousef needs data engineering resources to assist with mapping the Oracle profitability management schemas.
Confirm Randy's coverage plan for his upcoming mid-June family vacation.
rbvh41ja05sk3hr0lmv15v4h9k.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Randy Burris | Mark Francis

g0lh2httsasn1isocrjbsp9mhk.summary: You must prepare for organizational shifts stemming from the Tech Fellows offsite—where leadership positioned enterprise architects as high-level influencers rather than deep-dive engineers—and the restructured Horizontal Planning meeting format where horizontal teams present first and you primarily observe. Additionally, you need to manage Matt Unsworth's departure to Dan Weaver's team in two weeks—transitioning his responsibilities to Vince to maintain stability and keep him engaged. These changes highlight a broader push to align architecture value and address retention concerns.
tasks: Finalize the transition plan for Matt Unsworth's responsibilities to Vince—leveraging Vince's policy experience before Matt departs in two weeks.
Observe the new Horizontal Planning meeting structure on Thursday—noting how the horizontal teams present their horizons and dependencies first.

Establish the target operating state for enterprise architects as high-level ringleaders and influencers—realigning the team away from deep-dive engineering work per the Tech Fellows offsite directives.
Escalate the operational risk regarding Property and Casualty data management—addressing the severe skill gaps identified among Integration Architects.
g0lh2httsasn1isocrjbsp9mhk.title: Follow-up Full Staff

r0e5jqo24aho4bhrfvfhmlia14.summary: Retrieve your missing alignment points—your local work notes contain no record of recent discussions with Mike or Kathleen Ores Walsh. Ask Mike directly to reconstruct the critical outcomes of your last session—ensuring no continuity is lost. Establish a fresh communication baseline with Kathleen Ores Walsh—targeting her current operational bottlenecks.
tasks: Reconstruct the agenda from your last discussion with Mike—using your sent email history.
Request a status update from Kathleen Ores Walsh—focusing on immediate cross-functional dependencies.
Establish a permanent tracking document for Mike and Kathleen—preventing future retrieval failures in your work notes.
Confirm the bi-weekly meeting cadence with Mike—adjusting for upcoming schedule conflicts.
r0e5jqo24aho4bhrfvfhmlia14.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Mike Wilkins | Mark Francis
